Title: Default Layout - Variation Link Renders and Is Clickable (Slug Present)
Preconditions: 1. QA environment accessible at ${FRONT_URL}. 2. A product with variations in default (card/list) layout exists. 3. At least one variation has a slug configured in Admin catalog. 4. Guest browse (no login required).
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${FRONT_URL}
2. Navigate to the product page with default-layout variations (e.g., /products-with-options or direct product URL)
3. Scroll down to the Variations section
4. Hover the mouse cursor over a variation item (name or image area)
5. Note the href attribute of the variation link via status bar or right-click Inspect
6. Click on the variation item (name or image area)
7. Observe the loaded page URL in the browser address bar
8. Verify the product page content matches the clicked variation
9. Open browser DevTools (F12) Console tab
Expected Result:
- Variation item is rendered as a clickable link (cursor: pointer on hover)
- URL preview shows ${FRONT_URL}/{variation-slug}
- After click: URL is ${FRONT_URL}/{variation-slug} with no /product/ prefix
- Correct variation product page displays (name, images, properties match clicked variation)
- Zero JavaScript console errors

Title: Default Layout - Variation Link Renders Using ID Fallback (No Slug)
Preconditions: 1. A product with at least one variation that has NO slug configured in Admin catalog. 2. The variation has a valid id.
Steps:
1. Navigate to the parent product page containing the slug-less variation
2. Open browser DevTools Network tab, filter by graphql, find the products query response and note the variation id
3. Hover over the slug-less variation item in the default layout
4. Check href in status bar
5. Click on the slug-less variation item
6. Check the browser URL
7. Verify page content matches the variation
8. Check DevTools Console
Expected Result:
- Link href shows ${FRONT_URL}/product/{variation-id}
- After click: URL is ${FRONT_URL}/product/{variation-id} (ID-based route used)
- Correct variation product page loads
- No JavaScript console errors

Title: Table Layout (Desktop) - Variation Title Is a Clickable Link (Slug Present)
Preconditions: 1. Browser viewport at desktop width minimum 1280px (use 1920x1080). 2. A product page with ProductVariationsLayout property set to Table. 3. At least one variation has a slug configured.
Steps:
1. Navigate to the product page with table-layout variations at desktop viewport (1920x1080)
2. Locate the Item column header in the variations table
3. Hover over the variation name text in the first row of the Item column
4. Note the link href by checking status bar or right-click Inspect
5. Click on the variation name in the Item column
6. Verify browser URL
7. Verify page content is the correct variation
8. Press browser Back button
9. Check DevTools Console

Title: Table Layout (Desktop) - Variation Title Link Falls Back to ID Route (No Slug)
Preconditions: 1. Desktop viewport (>= 1280px). 2. Product with table layout containing a variation that has NO slug configured.
Steps:
1. Navigate to the product page with table layout
2. Identify the variation row for a slug-less variation (verify via Network tab GraphQL response if needed)
3. Hover over the variation name in that row
4. Click the variation name
5. Verify URL is /product/{variation-id}
6. Verify correct variation page content
7. Check DevTools Console

Title: Link Shareability - Copied URL Opens Correct Variation Page in New Session
Preconditions: 1. Product with at least 2 variations each with distinct slugs. 2. Access to a second browser window or incognito/private mode.
Steps:
1. Navigate to the parent product page (either layout) — variations with clickable links are visible
2. Right-click on the first variation link and select Copy link address
3. Paste the URL into a text editor to verify its format
4. Open a new incognito/private browser window (no cookies, no history)
5. Paste the copied URL into the incognito window address bar and press Enter
6. Verify the loaded page displays the correct variation
7. Repeat steps 2-6 for a second variation from the same product
Expected Result:
- Copied URL format is ${FRONT_URL}/{variation-slug} or ${FRONT_URL}/product/{id}
- Fresh-session page load (HTTP 200) displays the exact variation that was linked: name, images, SKU, description all match
- No redirect to parent product and no 404
- Second variation's direct URL also loads its own correct variation page

Title: Variation Link Opens in Same Browser Tab (Default Navigation Behavior)
Preconditions: 1. Product page with variations in either layout. 2. No special browserTarget new-tab configuration in store config.
Steps:
1. Navigate to product page with variations
2. Note the current number of open browser tabs (should be 1)
3. Left-click (standard click) on a variation link
4. Verify the number of open browser tabs
5. Verify browser Back button is available and returns to previous product page
Expected Result:
- Standard left-click navigates within the current tab (tab count stays at 1)
- Browser Back button is available and returns to the parent product page
- No unintended new tab opened on standard left-click

Title: Add-to-Cart Works Correctly — Link Does Not Intercept Cart Button Clicks
Preconditions: 1. Signed in as B2B user (USER_EMAIL/USER_PASSWORD) or browsing as guest. 2. Product page with variations in both default layout and table layout. 3. Cart is empty at start.
Steps:
1. Navigate to product page with variations in default layout
2. Locate the Add to Cart button or quantity stepper for the first variation
3. Set quantity to 1 (if stepper shown)
4. Click the Add to Cart button for the variation
5. Verify the current page URL has NOT changed
6. Navigate to product page with table layout variations
7. Click Add to Cart button in the table Quantity column for a variation
8. Verify page URL has NOT changed
9. Navigate to cart page (${FRONT_URL}/cart)
10. Verify both added variations appear in the cart
Expected Result:
- Success notification (toast) appears; cart icon badge increments by 1 for each Add to Cart action
- Page URL does NOT change after clicking Add to Cart — the click does NOT trigger variation page navigation
- Cart page shows both variations with correct names and quantities
- No console errors throughout

Title: Mobile - Default Layout Variation Links Render and Are Tappable
Preconditions: 1. Browser DevTools device emulation set to iPhone 14 (390x844) or physical mobile device. 2. Product page with variations in default layout. 3. Guest browse.
Steps:
1. Open ${FRONT_URL} on mobile viewport (390x844)
2. Navigate to the product page with default-layout variations
3. Scroll down to the Variations section
4. Inspect the visual layout of each variation card for mobile rendering
5. Tap on a variation item (name or image area — the linked zone)
6. Verify the variation page URL
7. Verify the variation page content
8. Tap browser Back button
9. Tap the Add to Cart button on a different variation (not the linked area)
Expected Result:
- Variations section is visible and scrollable; no overflow or broken layout on mobile
- Each variation card shows: image, name, properties, price, Add to Cart button — all readable without horizontal scroll
- Tap on name/image navigates to the variation product page (correct URL and content)
- Browser Back returns to parent product variations section
- Tap on Add to Cart does NOT trigger link navigation (tap targets are distinct)
- No layout breakage; no JS errors

Title: Table Layout - Mobile Viewport Renders Without Breaking
Section: Product Page / Variations - Table Layout Mobile
Preconditions: 1. Mobile viewport (390x844 or < 1024px width). 2. Product page with table layout variations.
Steps:
1. Open product page with table layout on mobile viewport (< 1024px wide)
2. Scroll to the Variations section
3. Verify which template VcTable uses on mobile (should be mobile/responsive state not desktop-body)
4. Verify variations are still displayed in a usable format
5. Inspect DOM via DevTools Elements tab for any malformed links
6. Check DevTools Console
7. Interact with any visible controls (e.g., Add to Cart)
Expected Result:
- VcTable renders its mobile/responsive state on viewports < 1024px; the #desktop-body slot (containing VcProductTitle :to) is NOT rendered on mobile
- Variations are displayed in a readable and usable mobile format
- No malformed anchor tags (no href='undefined' or href='')
- No JS errors or warnings in console
- Interactive controls (Add to Cart) work correctly on mobile

Title: Edge Case - Variation With Neither Slug Nor Valid ID Renders Without Broken Link
Preconditions: Edge case: a variation with empty/null id and no slug. This may require test data creation. If data not available execute via DOM inspection of existing variation pages.
Steps:
1. Navigate to a product page with variations (or create test data with a null-id variation if possible)
2. Open DevTools Elements tab and inspect the rendered HTML of variation items
3. Look for any anchor tags with href='undefined', href='null', href='#', or empty href
4. Verify variation items still display name, price, and Add to Cart correctly
5. Check DevTools Console for Vue prop warnings or JS errors
Expected Result:
- When getProductRoute returns undefined (no id and no slug), no clickable anchor tag is rendered or the route prop is absent/undefined — no broken href values
- Variation item still renders its content (name, price, Add to Cart) correctly even without a link
- No Vue warnings about invalid prop values for the route/to prop
- No JavaScript errors

Title: Regression - Core Product Page Features Are Unaffected
Preconditions: 1. Product page with variations accessible (both default and table layout). 2. Signed-in B2B user.
Steps:
1. Open product page with default-layout variations
2. Verify main product: name, hero images, description, SKU, price, Add to Cart all display
3. Verify breadcrumbs are correct (Home > Category > Product Name)
4. Verify each variation shows: image, name, property chips, list price, actual price, Add to Cart
5. Verify stock badges (In Stock / quantity / 0 out-of-stock) display correctly per variation
6. Navigate to product page with table layout variations
7. Verify table column headers: Item, property columns, Stock, Price, Quantity
8. Verify table rows show correct data for each variation
9. Click the Item column header to sort
10. Test pagination control if product has multiple pages of variations
11. Open DevTools Console throughout
Expected Result:
- All product page features work as before the PR
- Breadcrumbs, images, descriptions, prices all correct
- Stock badges match actual inventory
- Table sorting re-sorts rows without navigating away
- Pagination loads next set of variations; all remain clickable links
- Zero JS errors at any point

Title: URL Structure Verification - SEO Slug vs ID Route Confirmed via Network Tab
Preconditions: 1. Browser DevTools available. 2. Product page with variations including both a slug variation and a no-slug variation.
Steps:
1. Open browser DevTools Network tab, filter by graphql
2. Navigate to the parent product page
3. Find the products GraphQL request in Network tab and note variation slug and id values from the response
4. Hover over variation A (has slug) link in the layout
5. Click variation A link and check browser URL
6. Navigate back
7. Hover over variation B (no slug) link
8. Click variation B link and check browser URL
9. Verify neither URL triggers a 404 or redirect

Title: UX - Variation Link Visual Styling Is Consistent in Default Layout
Preconditions: 1. Desktop viewport (1920x1080). 2. Product page with variations in default layout.
Steps:
1. Navigate to product page with default-layout variations
2. Observe variation name and image in resting state (no hover)
3. Hover over the variation name
4. Hover over the variation image area
5. Hover over the Add to Cart button or stepper area
6. Take screenshots: resting state, hover-on-name state, hover-on-cart-button state
7. Compare visual styling against VcLineItem links elsewhere on the storefront (e.g., cart page, wishlist)
Expected Result:
- In resting state: variation items look consistent with other product cards on the storefront
- On hover over name/image: cursor shows pointer; visual feedback appears (color change or underline per Coffee theme)
- On hover over Add to Cart area: cursor shows pointer for the button — this is visually distinct from the navigation link area
- Styling is consistent with the VcLineItem component's link behavior in other contexts
- No jarring color flashes or unexpected underline behavior

Title: UX - Variation Title Link Styling Is Consistent in Table Layout
Section: Product Page / Variations - Table Layout UX
Preconditions: 1. Desktop viewport (>= 1280px). 2. Product page with table layout variations.
Steps:
1. Navigate to product page with table layout on desktop
2. Observe the Item column in resting state — compare styling with catalog product title links
3. Hover over a variation name in the Item column
4. Observe the other table columns (property values, Stock, Price)
5. Observe the Quantity column (Add to Cart stepper)
6. Take screenshots of table in resting and hover states
Expected Result:
- Item column variation names are styled as hyperlinks (consistent with VcProductTitle link behavior on catalog pages)
- On hover: cursor shows pointer; link hover effect applied (underline or color per Coffee theme)
- Other columns (properties, Stock, Price) show no pointer cursor and no link hover styling
- Quantity column (stepper and Add to Cart button) shows pointer only on interactive controls
- Overall visual style is consistent with catalog product title links

Title: Category Page - Variation Chip/Swatch Links Are Not Affected by This PR
Preconditions: 1. Category page that shows products with visible variation swatches or chips. 2. Navigate to /products-with-options or /accessories.
Steps:
1. Navigate to a category listing page with products that have variation swatches (e.g., ${FRONT_URL}/products-with-options)
2. Hover over a variation chip or swatch on a product card
3. Click a variation chip or swatch
4. Document the current behavior (swatch toggle vs navigation link)
5. Verify no regressions introduced by PR #2182 on the category page
Expected Result:
- This PR does NOT modify category page variation components
- Variation chips/swatches on category page behave the same as before this PR (document baseline: toggles card image OR navigates to variation PDP — whichever is current behavior)
- No regressions introduced on the category page
- Baseline behavior documented for follow-up work if category page links are in scope for a future story

Title: Cross-Browser (Firefox) - Default Layout Variation Links Work Correctly
Preconditions: 1. Firefox browser (latest 2 versions). 2. Product page with variations in default layout.
Steps:
1. Open ${FRONT_URL} in Firefox
2. Navigate to the product page with default-layout variations
3. Hover over a variation item
4. Click on a variation item link
5. Verify the URL and page content
6. Check Firefox browser console (F12 Console)
Expected Result:
- Variation link behavior in Firefox is identical to Chrome: cursor pointer on hover, correct URL after click, correct variation page loaded
- No Firefox-specific rendering issues
- No JS errors in Firefox console

Title: Cross-Browser (Edge) - Table Layout Variation Title Links Work Correctly
Preconditions: 1. Microsoft Edge browser (latest version). 2. Product page with table layout variations. 3. Desktop viewport (>= 1280px).
Steps:
1. Open ${FRONT_URL} in Edge
2. Navigate to product page with table layout variations at desktop viewport
3. Hover over a variation name in the Item column
4. Click on a variation name
5. Verify URL and page content
6. Check Edge DevTools console
Expected Result:
- Table layout variation title links work in Edge identically to Chrome: pointer cursor on hover, correct URL after click, correct variation page loaded
- No Edge-specific rendering issues
- No JS errors in Edge DevTools console

Title: Keyboard Accessibility - Variation Links Are Keyboard-Navigable
Preconditions: 1. Desktop viewport. 2. Product page with variations in default layout and/or table layout. 3. No mouse; keyboard-only interaction.
Steps:
1. Navigate to the product page
2. Press Tab repeatedly to move focus through the page toward the variations section
3. Tab until focus reaches a variation item link (name in default layout or name cell in table layout)
4. Verify focus indicator is visible on the variation link
5. Press Enter while variation link is focused
6. Press Alt+Left Arrow (browser Back) to return to parent product page
7. Tab to a variation's Add to Cart button
8. Press Enter or Space on Add to Cart button
Expected Result:
- Focus indicator (outline/ring) is visible on each variation link when focused
- Pressing Enter on a focused variation link navigates to the variation's product page (same as mouse click)
- Alt+Left Arrow (Back) returns to parent product page
- Add to Cart button is independently focusable via Tab and activatable via Enter or Space
- Add to Cart does NOT navigate to the variation page when activated via keyboard

Title: Browser History - Back and Forward Navigation After Visiting Variation Page
Preconditions: 1. Product page with at least 2 variations. 2. Standard desktop browser.
Steps:
1. Navigate to the parent product page and note the URL
2. Click on variation A link
3. Click Back button to return to parent product page
4. Click on variation B link
5. Press browser Back button
6. Press browser Forward button
7. Verify each history entry loads the correct page
8. Verify variations section on parent product is intact after navigating back
Expected Result:
- After clicking variation A: URL changes to variation A URL; browser has 2 history entries
- Back button returns to parent product page
- After clicking variation B: URL changes to variation B URL
- Back button returns to parent product page (not variation A)
- Forward button returns to variation B page
- Each page at each history state displays correct content
- Variations section on parent product renders correctly after back navigation (no stale or broken state)

Title: Pagination - Variation Links Are Clickable on All Pages of Paginated Variations
Preconditions: 1. A product with enough variations to trigger pagination (more than the default page size, typically > 10 variations). 2. If no such product exists in QA, mark as N/A with note.
Steps:
1. Navigate to the product page with paginated variations
2. Verify pagination control is visible at the bottom of the variations section
3. Verify each variation on page 1 has a clickable link (pointer cursor and valid href)
4. Click the pagination control to go to page 2
5. Verify each variation on page 2 also has a clickable link
6. Click on a variation link from page 2
7. Navigate back to the parent product
Expected Result:
- First page of variations renders with clickable links on all items
- Pagination control navigates to page 2 successfully
- Second page of variations also has clickable links on all items
- Clicking a variation link from page 2 navigates to the correct variation page
- Back navigation returns to the parent product (pagination may reset to page 1 — note actual behavior)

Title: No JavaScript Errors Throughout Full Variation Link Interaction Session
Preconditions: 1. Browser DevTools open from the start with Console tab visible. 2. Console set to All levels (not filtered). 3. Product page with variations accessible.
Steps:
1. Open DevTools Console then navigate to the parent product page with default-layout variations
2. Scroll through the entire product page including the variations section
3. Hover over multiple variation links in the default layout
4. Click a variation link and navigate to the variation page
5. Navigate back to the parent product page
6. Open a product page with table layout variations
7. Hover over the Item column variation titles
8. Click a table-layout variation title and navigate to the variation page then back
9. Click Add to Cart on a variation (default layout)
10. Review the full console log
Expected Result:
- Zero JavaScript errors at all steps
- Zero Vue warnings about invalid props (route, to) or undefined values
- No failed network requests (no 4xx or 5xx responses) triggered by link navigation
- No console errors during Add to Cart action
- No errors related to routing or vue-router during any navigation step

Title: Catalog List View - Switch to List Mode and Locate a Product That Has Variations
Preconditions: 1. QA environment accessible at ${FRONT_URL}. 2. A category page exists containing at least one product with hasVariations=true. 3. Guest browse (no login required).
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${FRONT_URL}
2. Navigate to a product category containing products with variations (e.g., ${FRONT_URL}/products-with-options or /e2e-catalog)
3. Locate the view-mode toggle control in the toolbar (near sort and filter controls)
4. Click the List view mode button
5. Scan the product list for a product that shows a Variations (N) button (e.g., Variations (3))
6. Verify the product card in list mode shows: product image, name as a link, properties, price with From label, and the Variations (N) button
7. Open browser DevTools Console tab
Expected Result:
- List view mode activates; products display in horizontal list layout
- At least one product card shows the Variations (N) button with a count
- Product card layout is correct in list mode (image, name, properties, price, variations button all rendered)
- No JavaScript console errors during view mode switch or list rendering

Title: Catalog List View - Variations Button Expands to Show Variation Line Items With Links
Preconditions: 1. Category page loaded in list view mode (follow TC-022 steps 1-4). 2. At least one product with variations is visible in list. 3. Variations have slugs configured in Admin catalog.
Steps:
1. In list view, locate a product card with a Variations (N) button (e.g., Variations (3))
2. Click the Variations (N) button
3. Observe the expanded variations section
4. Verify the title above the variation list (e.g., Available variations (3))
5. Hover over a variation item name or image area in the expanded section
6. Note the href attribute of the variation link by checking the status bar or right-click Inspect
7. Verify all visible variation line items have pointer cursor and a valid href
8. Open browser DevTools Console tab
Expected Result:
- Variations (N) button shows loading indicator briefly then expands to reveal variations section; button chevron changes to chevron-up
- Variations render as VcLineItem card/list rows — same component as PDP default layout — each showing image, name, properties, price, Add to Cart
- Section heading (Available variations (N)) is present with correct count
- Each variation item has cursor: pointer and href showing ${FRONT_URL}/{variation-slug} on hover
- No JavaScript errors during expansion or link rendering

Title: Catalog List View - Clicking Variation Link Navigates to the Correct Variation Product Page
Preconditions: 1. Category page loaded in list view mode. 2. A product with at least 2 variations each with distinct slugs is visible. 3. Variations section has been expanded (follow TC-023 steps 1-4).
Steps:
1. In the expanded variations section, identify two distinct variation line items (e.g., variation A: Blue - Size M; variation B: Red - Size L)
2. Note the name and distinguishing properties of variation A
3. Click on variation A name or image (the linked area)
4. Observe the browser URL after navigation
5. Verify the loaded page content matches variation A
6. Press the browser Back button
7. Expand the variations section again for the same product
8. Click on variation B link
9. Verify the loaded page content matches variation B
10. Open DevTools Console at any point
Expected Result:
- Clicking variation A navigates to ${FRONT_URL}/{variation-A-slug} — no /product/ prefix
- Loaded PDP content matches variation A specifically (name, images, SKU, properties)
- Back button returns to the category list page
- Variations section re-loads and renders with clickable links after returning
- Clicking variation B navigates to ${FRONT_URL}/{variation-B-slug} — a different URL from variation A
- Correct variation B PDP content loads
- No JavaScript errors throughout

Title: Catalog List View - Variation Link URL Uses Slug Route When Available Falls Back to ID Route
Section: Catalog / List View - Variations - URL Correctness
Preconditions: 1. Category page loaded in list view mode. 2. A product is available with one variation that has a slug configured (variation A) and one variation without slug (variation B). 3. Expand the variations section for that product.
Steps:
1. Open DevTools Network tab, filter by graphql
2. Click Variations (N) button for the target product in list view
3. In the Network tab, click the GraphQL request for the product variations and find the variations array in the response
4. Note: variation A has a non-empty slug; variation B has empty or absent slug
5. Hover over variation A link in the expanded section
6. Click variation A link and check browser URL
7. Navigate back to the category list and re-expand variations
8. Hover over variation B link (no slug)
9. Click variation B link and check browser URL
10. Verify neither URL results in a 404 or redirect
Expected Result:
- GraphQL response confirms slug and id values for each variation
- Variation A (has slug): hover shows ${FRONT_URL}/{slug}; after click URL = ${FRONT_URL}/{slug} — no /product/ prefix
- Variation B (no slug): hover shows ${FRONT_URL}/product/{id}; after click URL = ${FRONT_URL}/product/{id}
- Both pages return HTTP 200 with correct variation content
- URL routing behavior is consistent with PDP context for the same variations

Title: Catalog List View - Browser Back Button Returns to Category List After Visiting Variation Page
Preconditions: 1. Category page loaded in list view mode. 2. A product with at least one variation (slug present) is visible and its variations section can be expanded. 3. Guest browse.
Steps:
1. Navigate to the category page in list view and note the current URL
2. Expand the Variations (N) section for a product
3. Click on a variation link
4. Verify the variation product page loads fully
5. Press the browser Back button (or Alt+Left Arrow)
6. Verify the category list page URL is restored
7. Verify the category list page is still in list view mode (not reset to grid)
8. Open DevTools Console
Expected Result:
- Browser navigates to ${FRONT_URL}/{variation-slug}; browser history has 2 entries
- Back button navigates back to the category list page; URL matches the category URL noted in step 1
- Category list remains in list view mode — not reverted to grid
- No JavaScript errors during navigation sequence

Title: Catalog List View - Mobile Viewport: Variation Expansion and Link Behavior
Preconditions: 1. Browser DevTools device emulation set to iPhone 14 (390x844) or equivalent narrow viewport. 2. Category page accessible with at least one product with variations.
Steps:
1. Open ${FRONT_URL}/e2e-catalog (or equivalent) on mobile viewport (390x844)
2. Observe the catalog view mode and what button/control is shown for variations on mobile
3. Locate a product with variations and tap the variations button visible on mobile
4. Verify behavior: on narrow viewports (below 3xl container breakpoint) the expand-in-place Variations button is hidden; the product-card__variations-link-button is shown and navigates to the parent product PDP
5. Navigate back to the category list
6. Switch to desktop viewport (1920x1080 via DevTools)
7. Confirm the expand-in-place Variations (N) button with chevron appears at desktop breakpoint
8. Expand variations at desktop viewport and verify variation links are clickable
9. Check DevTools Console throughout
Expected Result:
- On mobile (below 3xl container breakpoint): the link button navigates to the parent product PDP (expected design behavior)
- On desktop (at or above 3xl breakpoint): the expand-in-place button works; expanded variations have pointer cursor and valid hrefs
- No layout breakage at any viewport (no overflow, no unreadable variation items)
- No JavaScript errors at any viewport

Title: Catalog List View - Add-to-Cart in Expanded Variations Does Not Trigger Navigation Link
Preconditions: 1. Category page loaded in list view mode at desktop viewport (>= 1280px; ensure 3xl container breakpoint is met). 2. A product with variations is visible; variations section has been expanded. 3. At least one variation is in stock. 4. Signed in as B2B user (USER_EMAIL/USER_PASSWORD) or guest. 5. Cart is empty at start.
Steps:
1. In the expanded variations section of a product card in catalog list view, locate an in-stock variation
2. Note the current page URL (should be category page URL)
3. Set quantity to 1 if stepper is shown
4. Click the Add to Cart button for the variation
5. Verify the page URL has NOT changed
6. Verify the category list is still visible with the variations section still expanded
7. Navigate to the cart page (${FRONT_URL}/cart)
8. Verify the variation just added appears in the cart with correct product name, quantity, and price
9. Open DevTools Console throughout
Expected Result:
- Success notification (toast) appears; cart icon badge increments by 1
- Page URL does NOT change after clicking Add to Cart — the click does NOT navigate to the variation page
- Category list page and expanded variations section remain intact
- Cart page shows the correct variation with correct name, quantity, and price
- No JavaScript errors throughout

Title: Catalog List View - Zero JavaScript Errors During Full Variation Expand and Link Navigation Session
Preconditions: 1. Browser DevTools open from start with Console tab visible and set to All levels. 2. Category page accessible with products that have variations. 3. Desktop viewport (1920x1080) to ensure expand-in-place variations button is visible.
Steps:
1. Open DevTools Console then navigate to the category page
2. Switch to list view mode using the view mode toggle
3. Scroll through the list observing product cards with variations buttons
4. Click the Variations (N) button to expand the first product variations
5. Hover over each variation link in the expanded section
6. Click a variation link and navigate to the variation PDP
7. Navigate back to the category list
8. Expand variations for a second product if available
9. Click Add to Cart on a variation from the expanded section
10. Collapse the variations section by clicking the Variations (N) button again
11. Review the full console log
Expected Result:
- Zero JavaScript errors at all steps
- Zero Vue warnings about undefined route/to props or invalid VcLineItem :route prop
- No failed network requests (no 4xx or 5xx) related to variation data loading or navigation
- No errors during Add to Cart action
- No errors during expand or collapse of the variations section
